instant.evidence: Design-time validation persona; runs gates on demand, not a latency-bound service.

natural.evidence: Flags specific failures in plain language with chapter/section references.

permitted.evidence: Binary gate authority; partial success is not sufficient; never marks a gate passed when failures exist.

adaptive.evidence: Deterministic checklist validator; gate rules change only via approval-gated directive edits (CLAUDE.md), not autonomous learning.

contextual.evidence: Validates each chapter against the outline and the project's Requirement set.

transparent.evidence: Per-gate results recorded via state_manager.record_final_quality(); failures cite exact location.

governance.evidence: Enforces the gate sequence; never skips a gate; partial success rejected.

observability.evidence: Quality report with per-gate results is persisted.

availability.evidence: On-demand validation tool, not an always-on service.

lexicon.evidence: Directly enforces consistent terminology (Gate 2 Clarity) and anti-vagueness.

solid.evidence: Backed by deterministic quality_gate_runner, ambiguity_detector, outline_validator; covered by /tests.

layers.name: Observability & Feedback
layers.how: Per-gate quality report persisted via state_manager.
